anchors.1: The criterion is contradicted or materially absent.
anchors.3: The criterion is present but uneven, conventional, or weakly evidenced.
anchors.5: The criterion is clear, purposeful, and supported by the rendered artifact.

dimensions.name: Content fit
dimensions.question: Does the composition arise from this brief's audience, task, and content relationships?

dimensions.name: Structure honesty
dimensions.question: Does visible geometry clarify hierarchy, sequence, comparison, status, or action?

dimensions.name: Inspectability
dimensions.question: Can a reader explain where every measure, label, state, and technical mark comes from?

dimensions.name: Useful delight
dimensions.question: Is there at most one restrained expressive system, and does it improve clarity, attention, memory, or ease?

dimensions.name: Access and resilience
dimensions.question: Do semantic order, keyboard use, focus, narrow reflow, reduced motion, and the no-JavaScript form preserve meaning?

dimensions.name: Authored identity
dimensions.question: Does the result express editorial warmth, technical precision, and direct construction without becoming a generic brutalist theme?

dimensions.name: Adaptive composition
dimensions.question: Does the result avoid copying the reference-site shell or applying JBM forms without a brief-specific reason?

smells.name: Motif without a job
smells.description: A visible JBM-associated form appears without clarifying content, structure, state, or action.

smells.name: Decorative pseudo-data
smells.description: The artifact invents measurements, coordinates, status, progress, charts, diagnostics, or scientific-looking labels.

smells.name: Gratuitous brutalism
smells.description: Borders, type, contrast, density, or friction become aggressive without helping the task.

smells.name: Unearned scale
smells.description: Display type remains unusually large even though reducing it would preserve hierarchy, comprehension, and experience.

smells.name: Effect stacking
smells.description: Multiple unrelated delights or atmospheric effects compete for attention.

smells.name: Generic kit expansion
smells.description: The artifact becomes a catalog of familiar controls or cards that the supplied content does not require.

smells.name: Reference-template copy
smells.description: The reference site's hero, reading rail, Plate grid, or figure composition is reproduced without a brief-specific reason.

smells.name: Technical voice overreach
smells.description: Instrument typography or notation overtakes long reading, human context, or ordinary utility text.

smells.name: Inaccessible delight
smells.description: Meaning or operation depends on hover, pointer precision, animation, color alone, or JavaScript.

motifs.name: Dominant display hero
motifs.description: The opening viewport is led by unusually large display type.

motifs.name: Sticky side rail
motifs.description: A persistent lateral region carries navigation, status, or measure.

motifs.name: Bordered module grid
motifs.description: Several bounded modules form a repeated grid.

motifs.name: Numbered ledger
motifs.description: Repeated records use visible indexes or folios.

motifs.name: Technical diagram
motifs.description: Rules, nodes, axes, or labels explain a real relationship or dataset.

motifs.name: Measured progress
motifs.description: The artifact exposes real progress, capacity, position, or completion.

motifs.name: Paper offset shadow
motifs.description: A crisp offset or inset mark expresses paper depth.

motifs.name: Notebook grid
motifs.description: A ruled or gridded Ground structures the composition.

motifs.name: Stamp or seal
motifs.description: A compact stamped, sealed, or ringed mark carries a real state or action.

motifs.name: Mono kicker system
motifs.description: Compact monospaced orientation labels recur across sections.

motifs.name: Split editorial layout
motifs.description: Two or more visibly unequal columns create editorial tension.
